text.title: QA Rollback Trigger Canon Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Outage at 02:00; on-call debates 'is 1% errors enough to rollback?' for 30 min.
antipattern.fix: Lock triggers before release; if absent, default to most conservative SLO and roll back.

antipattern.symptom: Trigger 'high error rate' has no metric path.
antipattern.fix: Translate to Prometheus expression + selector; reject any vague trigger.

antipattern.symptom: Trigger fires → opens Jira → ack 6h later → still no rollback.
antipattern.fix: Wire trigger to PagerDuty + one-click rollback button; remove ticket route.
